risk_event: #  RISK OBSERVATIONS  —  FOR THE BAYESIAN UPDATE TAB (OPTIONAL)
#  Use this file in the sidebar's "Risk observations CSV" upload
#  (Bayesian update section). Optional — only needed if you also want
#  to update risk-event probabilities
#  WHAT TO DO
#  1. DELETE the example rows at the bottom of this file.
#  2. ADD one row per risk event you tracked in the new campaign.
#  3. risk_event must match (partially
#     in master_risks_assumptions.csv
#     delay"
#  4. n_trials / n_events depend on the risk's Scope in the
#     assumptions file:
#       stage    scope -> n_trials = total stages pumped this campaign
#       well     scope -> n_trials = total wells in this campaign
#       campaign scope -> n_trials = 1 (it either happened or it didn't)
#     n_events = number of times the risk actually occurred.
#  The Bayesian Update tab combines these counts with the assumptions
#  CSV's prior probability (Beta-Binomial update) and reports whether
#  each risk is Stable / Increasing / Decreasing
#  strength (weak/moderate/strong) based on how tight the posterior is.
#  EXAMPLE DATA — replace with your actual new-campaign risk counts.
#  53 stages and 8 wells pumped this campaign (matches the example
#  new_campaign_wells_template.csv).
